title: Quantitative Reasoning, Estimation, and Problem Framing · Retrieval Circuit
summary: Estimate attendance, supplies, time, and uncertainty for a neighborhood event before exact values are available. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a transparent event-planning estimate notebook after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Interpret a number together with its unit, reference population, time window, and practical meaning.
Estimate magnitudes with benchmarks and defend an appropriate tolerance before calculating exactly.
Translate a practical question into known quantities, unknown quantities, constraints, and operations.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.
